title: Rich Smoky Canadian Cheese Casserole
description: A cozy Canadian dish featuring smoked paprika and a dairy-free friendly cheese alternative, perfect for a cold winter night. This casserole is en-croute, with a crispy crust giving way to a rich, cheesy interior.

ingredients.name: smoked paprika
ingredients.notes: use high-quality smoked paprika for best flavor

instructions: Preheat the oven to 375 degrees Fahrenheit.
In a large skillet, heat the olive oil over medium heat.
Add the onion powder, salt, and black pepper to the skillet and stir to combine.
Add the frozen peas and carrots to the skillet and cook until thawed.
In a large bowl, combine the dairy-free cheese shreds, canned corn, and cooked peas and carrots.
In a separate bowl, whisk together the gluten-free flour and smoked paprika.
Add the flour mixture to the cheese mixture and stir until well combined.
Transfer the mixture to a 9x13 inch baking dish.
Bake the casserole for 35 minutes, or until the crust is golden brown and the interior is hot and bubbly.
